title: Improved method for enhancing filtration yields in tangential flow filtration system
abstract: The disclosure generally relates to methods and apparatus for the efficient quantitative recovery of valuable biological fluids from filtration systems, more particularly to efficient quantitative recovery of valuable biological fluids from high precision separation systems suitable for use in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries.
claims:
Claims 1 . A process for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System (20) comprising: a. inducing an internalized (integrated) pressurized Flow throughout the Crossflow Filtration System by activating an Internal Pump (4), such as a Peristaltic pump, operably linked to a Sterile Air Source (5) at one end of the Crossflow Filtration System, b. recovering said Biological Fluid at a Drain Valve (6) operably linked at the opposite end of the Crossflow Filtration System. 2. The process according to claim 1 , wherein said Internal Pump is a Peristaltic pump. 3. The process according to claim 1 or 2, wherein said Crossflow Filtration System comprises a series of tubes, valves, sensors, and conduits connected together to form a Flowpath. 4. The process according to any preceding claim , wherein said Internal Pump operably linked to a Sterile Air Source is located near the terminus of the Crossflow Filtration System adjacent to a Biofluid Source Container (1 a). 5. The process according to any one of claims 1 -3, wherein said Internal Pump operably linked to a Sterile Air Source is located at the terminus of the Crossflow Filtration System adjacent to a Biofluid Source Container. 6. The process according to any preceding claim, wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized Flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 10-10,000 rpm. 7. The process according to any preceding claim, wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 100-1000 rpm. 8. The process according to any preceding claim, wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow output of from 1 mL/min to 1 L/min. 9. The process according to claim 8, wherein said Internal Pump pressurizes flow between 1 psi to 150 psi. 10. The process according to claim 8, wherein said Internal Pump pressurizes flow between 14 to 100 psi. 1 1 . The process according to any preceding claim, wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 14 to 50 psi. 12. The process according to any preceding claim for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said Sterile Air source is supplied passively or at a rate from about 1 to 10 liters per minute. 13. The process according to any preceding claim for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow at about 2 liters per minute. 14. The process according to any preceding claim for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is measured as a percentage of pump flow rate from 1 to100% of Flowrate. 15. The process according to any preceding claim for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow air is supplied passively. 16. The process according to any preceding claim for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is about 25% of maximum flow rate. 17. A system for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from a Crossflow Filtration System (alternatively wherein permeate flow has already been separated) wherein said Crossflow Filtration System (20) comprises: i. a Biofluid Container (1 a), ii. a Crossflow Filtration Filter (3), iii. a Feed Flow Line (1 1 ) operably linked from said Biofluid Container to said Crossflow Filtration Filter containing Feed Flow, iv. a Retentate Flow Line Return (12) operably linked from said Crossflow Filtration Filter to said Biofluid Container; said Retentate Flow Line Return containing unFiltered Flow, v. a Permeate Flow Line (13) operably linked to said Crossflow Filtration Filter containing (filtered) Permeate Flow; vi. an Internal Pump (4) operably linked to said Retentate Flow Line Return and a Sterile Air Source (5), vii. a Drain Line (14) operably linked (Valve) to said Feed Flow Line; 

 viii. wherein said Internal Pump is activated pressurizing Flow throughout said Crossflow filtration System from the operably linked Pump/Sterile Air Source to said Drain Valve (6) wherein said Flow is recovered. 18. The system according to claim 17 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from a Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized Flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 10-10,000 rpm. 19. The system according to claim 17 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 100-1000 rpm. 20. The system according to any one of claims 17-19 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow output of from 1 mL/min to 1 L/min. 21 . The system according to claim 20 wherein said air pressure is standard pressure (14 psi) to 150 psi. 22. The system according to any one of claims 17-21 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said Internal Pump pressurizes flow between 14 to 10Opsi. 23. The system according to any one of claims 17-21 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized Flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow between 14 to 50psi. 24. The system according to any one of claims 17-23 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said Sterile Air is supplied at a rate from about 1 to 10 liters per minute. 25. The system according to claim 24 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is activated by an Internal Pump pressuring flow at about 2 liters per minute. 26. The system according to any one of claims 17-25 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said induction of an internalized pressurized flow is measured as a percentage of pump flowrate from 1 to100% of flowrate. 27. The system according to any one of claims 17-26 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said Sterile Air is supplied passively. 28. The system according to any one of claims 17-27 for enhanced recovery of a Biological Fluid from an activated Crossflow Filtration System wherein said Sterile Air is pressurized at about 25% of maximum flowrate.
